log.why: Barrier blocking needs typed source categories so attacks, effects, objects, energy, and spell effects are not collapsed into description prose.

fields.comment: Stores what source categories are blocked by a barrier damage-prevention rule. It maps from the structured markdown label `Barrier Damage Block Source Categories` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].barrierDamagePrevention.sourceCategories[] records what kinds of harm cannot cross the barrier. Omit only when the effect has no barrier damage-prevention mechanic.

log.why: Source-side spell-effect prevention needs runtime data distinct from ordinary damage prevention.

fields.comment: Stores which side a blocked spell must originate from. It maps from the structured markdown label `Spell Effect Prevention Source Side` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].spellEffectPrevention.sourceSide keeps Globe-style outside-spell blocking explicit.

fields.structuredLabel: Spell Effect Prevention Max Spell Level

log.why: Level-gated spell blocking needs a base maximum spell level in runtime data.

fields.comment: Stores the highest spell level blocked at the base casting level. It maps from the structured markdown label `Spell Effect Prevention Max Spell Level` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].spellEffectPrevention.maxSpellLevel makes level-gated spell negation machine-readable.

log.why: The runtime needs to know what subjects a blocked spell cannot affect.

fields.comment: Stores what subjects qualifying spells cannot affect. It maps from the structured markdown label `Spell Effect Prevention Affected Subjects` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].spellEffectPrevention.affectedSubjects[] keeps the protected scope explicit.

fields.structuredLabel: Spell Effect Prevention Excludes Area Of Effect

log.why: Area exclusion is part of Globe of Invulnerability and should not be flattened into target immunity prose.

fields.comment: Stores whether qualifying spell areas exclude the protected area. It maps from the structured markdown label `Spell Effect Prevention Excludes Area Of Effect` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].spellEffectPrevention.excludesAreaOfEffect preserves area-of-effect exclusion separately from target-effect blocking.

log.why: Damage mirroring needs a runtime trigger separate from ordinary damage effects.

fields.comment: Stores which linked damage event causes mirrored damage. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Trigger` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.trigger keeps Warding Bond-style damage sharing machine-readable.

log.why: Damage mirroring needs to name who receives the mirrored damage.

fields.comment: Stores who takes the mirrored damage. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Recipient` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.recipient preserves the direction of linked damage sharing.

log.why: Damage mirroring needs an explicit amount rule instead of prose-only same-amount wording.

fields.comment: Stores how much damage is mirrored. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Amount` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.amount distinguishes mirrored damage from independent damage rolls or reductions.

log.why: Linked damage needs a runtime policy for whether it mirrors pre-mitigation or post-mitigation damage.

fields.comment: Stores which damage total is mirrored by the linked damage effect. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Amount Basis` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.amountBasis makes Warding Bond-style same-amount damage resolve from the final amount the protected target actually took instead of leaving resistance/vulnerability timing implicit. Omit only when the effect has no linked-damage mechanic.

log.why: Linked damage needs an explicit damage-type source so runtime logs and later mechanics do not infer type from prose.

fields.comment: Stores where the linked damage's type comes from. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Type Source` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.damageTypeSource preserves whether mirrored damage follows the triggering damage type or has no type. Omit only when the effect has no linked-damage mechanic.

log.why: Linked damage needs a runtime policy for whether the recipient's own resistance or vulnerability can modify the mirrored amount.

fields.comment: Stores whether the linked recipient applies their own resistance, vulnerability, or prevention after the mirrored amount is selected. It maps from the structured markdown label `Linked Damage Recipient Mitigation` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].linkedDamage.recipientMitigation prevents Warding Bond-style same-amount damage from being accidentally halved, doubled, or prevented a second time. Omit only when the effect has no linked-damage mechanic.

log.why: Condition suppression needs a runtime field separate from condition immunity and status removal.

fields.comment: Stores named status conditions that a defensive effect temporarily suppresses without removing. It maps from the structured markdown label `Condition Suppressions` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].conditionSuppression[] preserves pause-style condition mechanics such as Calm Emotions without flattening them into prose or pretending they are permanent immunity/removal. Omit or use an empty array only when the defensive effect has no condition-suppression mechanic.

log.why: Immediate condition-ending effects need a runtime field separate from condition application, immunity, and suppression.

fields.comment: Stores named status conditions that an effect ends immediately. It maps from the structured markdown label `Conditions Removed` after that label has been checked against canonical prose. Purpose: effects[].conditionRemoval[] preserves restorative mechanics such as Protection from Poison ending Poisoned without flattening them into utility prose. Omit or use an empty array only when the effect has no condition-removal mechanic.

log.why: Source-limited condition immunity needs a runtime source filter attached to the defensive immunity effect.

fields.comment: Stores the creature-type source filter for condition-immunity effects. It maps from the structured markdown label `Condition Immunity Source Creature Types`; use it only when the canonical prose says the target is immune to conditions from a named source group. Purpose: effects[].attackerFilter.creatureTypes[] preserves source-limited immunity without inventing a separate filter object for the same defensive source concept already used by attack-disadvantage effects.

log.why: Mapped from structured Attack Roll Modifier.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Attack Roll Modifier. It maps to `effects[].attackRollModifier.modifier`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Attack Roll Modifier`. Purpose: effects[].attackRollModifier.modifier exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Attack Roll Modifier`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].attackRollModifier.modifier`. The value type is `enum`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Omit or leave empty only when this runtime/template layer intentionally treats the field as optional.

acceptedValueNotes.advantage: Allowed value for effects[].attackRollModifier.modifier; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.disadvantage: Allowed value for effects[].attackRollModifier.modifier; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
